statement: The paper papanokechi/khinchin-signature-pslq (Papanokechi, April 2026 draft, intended Experimental Mathematics) reports a precision-controlled null PSLQ result for the Khinchin-signature coordinate family {S_n, log S_n, S_n - K_0, log n} on π's continued-fraction partial quotients, tested at 300-dps working / 600-dps confirmation with maxcoeff=10^3 over 6 fixed 3D/4D templates on the stability-certified prefix n=1..91; 546 conservative-cap attempts, all unsupported. The Discussion section explicitly identifies as out-of-scope-but-open: 'templates involving n^(1/2), n^2, or algebraic combinations of K_0 with other known constants lie entirely outside the tested family and remain open.' This passage is the legitimacy anchor for the present unsolved-relay mission's bounded sub-question on the hybrid basis B_D(C) per targets/selected.md §2.A.
reproduce_command: gh api repos/papanokechi/khinchin-signature-pslq/contents/paper/khinchin_signature_expmath_note.tex --header 'Accept: application/vnd.github.raw' (fetched at 2026-05-15 19:50 JST). Then code/run_pslq_sweep.py --input artifacts/stable_prefix.json --output artifacts/status_blocks/pslq_attempts_reproduced.json

independent_verifier_result.notes: Full TeX source read via GitHub MCP get_file_contents at 2026-05-15 ~19:50 JST. Discussion section quoted verbatim. Status reflects April 2026 draft pre-Zenodo-DOI state. --- H9 verification_class: field_standard_practice. The cited scope-distinction passage from the signature paper's Discussion section is a literature handoff (not a quantitative bound); classified as community-validated empirical practice (operator's own prior published work). Load-bearing on M2.3 predicate via EF2 (signature-paper-grandfathered S_n family).

statement: Bailey, Borwein, and Crandall (1997), 'On the Khintchine Constant', Mathematics of Computation 66 (1997), 417-431 (DOI 10.1090/S0025-5718-97-00800-4), computed K_0 to 7350 decimal digits using a fast-converging series involving zeta values and conducted two PSLQ-based integer-relation tests on K_0 and K_{-1} in §4. Test 1 (pure-power algebraicity): 'if K_0 satisfies a polynomial of the form 0 = a_0 + a_1 α + a_2 α^2 + a_3 α^3 + ... + a_50 α^50 in the variable α, then the magnitude of some integer coefficient a_k exceeds 10^70. The same was found to be true for K_{-1}.' Test 2 (log-multiplicative): 'neither K_0 nor K_{-1} satisfies a relation of the form 0 = a_0 log α + Σ_{k=1}^{15} a_k log p_k + a_16 log π + a_17 log e + a_18 log γ + a_19 log ζ(3) + a_20 log log 2 with integer coefficients a_k of absolute value 10^20 or less.' Both tests null. Open problems explicitly named at §4 close: 'further work might be to rule out the possibility that log K_0, (log K_0)(log 2), or one of many other forms involving K_0 be an algebraic number of low degree.' The operator's M1.2 sub-question (per targets/selected.md §2) extends BBC 1997 specifically along the log K_0 and bilinear K_0·c axes that BBC explicitly flagged as untested.

test2_basis_elements: log p_1 through log p_15 (first 15 primes)
open_problems_named: log K_0 algebraicity at low degree

statement: OEIS sequence A002210 records the decimal expansion of Khinchin's constant K_0 = 2.685452001065306445309714835481795693820382293994462953051152345557218..., currently listing 110 digits in the b-file. Carles Simó (Oct 2016) reports having computed 10^6 terms of the sequence; this is the standing computational record per OEIS' editorial note. The sequence cross-references BBC 1997 (Math. Comp. 66, 417-431), Shanks-Wrench 1959 (AMM 66, 276-279), and Wieting 2008 (Proc. AMS 136, 815-824).
reproduce_command: curl https://oeis.org/A002210  # web_fetch retrieved 2026-05-15 19:30 JST

independent_verifier_result.notes: OEIS page fetched and 110-digit value cross-checked against mpmath.khinchin at 500 dps (digits 1-100 match exactly per the env/M1.lock retroactive verification 2026-05-15). --- H9 verification_class: field_standard_practice. OEIS A002210 numerical value of K_0 is the field's canonical numerical record (cross-checked against mpmath.khinchin at 500 dps per M2.2 capability audit). Load-bearing on M2.3 predicate as the K_0 value used in the B_D(C) basis.

statement: The English Wikipedia article 'Khinchin's constant' (revision retrieved 2026-05-15) summarizes that Khinchin proved that for almost all real x, the geometric mean of partial-quotient sequence (a_1·a_2·...·a_n)^(1/n) converges to K_0 = 2.685452..., a constant independent of x. The article explicitly states: 'Although almost all numbers satisfy this property, it has not been proven for any real number not specifically constructed for the purpose.' Empirical conjectures (not theorems) hold for π, ln(2), γ, ζ(3), Feigenbaum constants, and K_0 itself. Excluded by theorem: rationals, quadratic irrationals, e. The article presents the Hölder-mean family K_p = [Σ_{k=1}^∞ -k^p log_2(1 - 1/(k+1)^2)]^(1/p), finite for p < 1, integral and Hurwitz-zeta representations.

precision_or_dependencies.primary_claims_aggregated: Khinchin theorem statement, K_0 numerical value, Hölder-mean K_p family, ergodic-theory proof via Gauss-Kuzmin map, almost-all property

reproduce_command: curl https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khinchin%27s_constant  # web_fetch 2026-05-15 19:30 JST

NOT a primary source for any computational claim entering the M3.1 harness. Note: Wikipedia does not mention BBC 1997's specific PSLQ test of K_0 — namespace tension surfaced; see lit-018.

statement: The Wikipedia 'Integer relation algorithm' article (retrieved 2026-05-15) lists the major integer-relation algorithms (LLL 1982, HJLS 1986, PSOS 1988, PSLQ 1992-Ferguson-Bailey, simplified 1999-Ferguson-Bailey-Arno) and gives a worked example of PSLQ's role in the Bailey-Borwein-Plouffe formula for π. The article does NOT mention any K_0-specific PSLQ work, despite BBC 1997's published §4 PSLQ tests on K_0. This is a tertiary-aggregator coverage gap, not a literature gap.

statement: Shanks and Wrench (1959), 'Khintchine's constant', American Mathematical Monthly 66, pp. 276-279, provided the first published high-precision numerical computation of K_0 using a convergent series formulation. This paper is cited in BBC 1997 reference [20] and listed in OEIS A002210 cross-references as JSTOR 2309633. BBC 1997 §5 uses Shanks-Wrench's series formulation as the starting point for their fast-converging series; BBC's 7350-digit computation is in 'complete agreement with Gosper's 2217 digits' (BBC §5), and presumably with Shanks-Wrench's smaller record.
reproduce_command: https://www.jstor.org/stable/2309633  # JSTOR; requires institutional access

independent_verifier_result.paywall_blocker: JSTOR institutional access required; first page abstract accessible but full text not available to this session
independent_verifier_result.notes: Substantive content confirmed transitively via BBC 1997 reference [20] (which itself is paper-read-verified). Specific numerical precision Shanks-Wrench achieved is unconfirmed without paper read.

statement: Khinchin, A. Ya. (1935), 'Metrische Kettenbruchprobleme', Compositio Mathematica 1, pp. 361-382, contains the original statement and proof of Khinchin's theorem: for almost all (in Lebesgue measure) real numbers x, the geometric mean of the partial quotients in x's regular continued fraction expansion converges to a universal constant K_0 = exp(Σ_{r=1}^∞ ln(1 + 1/(r(r+2))) log_2 r). This is the foundational citation for the entire mission. The original proof did not use ergodic theory (which was developed later); the modern proof via the Gauss-Kuzmin-Wirsing transfer operator is due to Ryll-Nardzewski (1951) — see lit-011.
reproduce_command: https://eudml.org/doc/88557  # EuDML free archive of Compositio Math 1935; visit and download PDF

independent_verifier_result.notes: EuDML hosts Compositio Mathematica back issues; not retrieved in this session. Statement confirmed transitively via Wikipedia (lit-004), OEIS A002210 (lit-003), and BBC 1997 (lit-002) all of which cite this paper as the foundational reference. Verification path: read the original paper via EuDML before any M3.1 harness step depends on a specific numerical or symbolic claim from this paper.

statement: Khinchin, A. Ya. (1963 / English translation 1964), 'Continued Fractions', P. Noordhoff Ltd., Groningen (English translation by Eisele, Wirth, Wynn at Univ. Chicago Press, 1964). Standard monograph on continued fractions; contains Khinchin's theorem (Chapter 3 in some editions) and historical commentary on the K_0 constant. OEIS A002210 lists this as a standard reference. Operator's prior probe (capability/machinery_base_confirmed.md) does not list this book as locally available.
reproduce_command: Cite as standard reference; consult Dover 1997 reprint ISBN 0-486-69630-8 for accessibility

independent_verifier_result.notes: Book reference; no digital access in this session. Operator may have physical copy or library access. Transitively confirmed via lit-002, lit-004, lit-007.

statement: Ferguson, Bailey, and Arno (1999), "Analysis of PSLQ, an integer relation finding algorithm", Mathematics of Computation 68 (1999), pp. 351-369, presents the definitive simplified PSLQ algorithm and proves: (Theorem 1) the per-iteration rigorous lower-bound certificate M_x >= 1 / max_i |h_{i,i}(k)| on the smallest possible relation norm; (Theorem 3) any PSLQ-found relation m has norm |m| <= gamma^{n-2} M_x for gamma > 2/sqrt(3) in the real case; (Corollary 2) PSLQ(tau) terminates in < (n choose 2) * log_tau(gamma^{n-1} M_x) iterations. The folklore relation "H ~ 10^(P/n)" between working precision P and certified absence of integer relations with coefficient height <= H is an empirical scaling of max_i |h_{i,i}| against P at typical PSLQ convergence; it is consistent with FBA 1999 framework but is NOT stated as a theorem in the paper. BBC 1997 (lit-002) empirically used a c ~ 2.06 confidence factor over the folklore heuristic: at n=51, P=7350 dps, they reported H = 10^70 versus 10^(7350/51) ~ 10^144 from the bare folklore. The harness M3.1 confidence-claim therefore inherits empirical_heuristic class (not rigorous_theorem class) unless a future implementation exposes max_i |h_{i,i}| directly per Theorem 1.

precision_or_dependencies.folklore_heuristic_not_in_paper: H ~ 10^(P/n) is empirical scaling, NOT a stated theorem

independent_verifier_result.notes: Paper retrieved 2026-05-15 ~21:30 JST from Bailey archive (cpslq.pdf preprint, identical title + authors + abstract with Math.Comp. 1999 journal version per page-1 "Ref: Mathematics of Computation, to appear (1999)" header). 218,997 bytes; 26 pages; pypdf extraction 51,322 chars. Theorem 1, Theorem 3, Corollary 2, Definition 5 (gamma/tau params), and Section 8 Computer Implementation all paper-read. M2.3 U-MISSION-K halt-class finding surfaced: previous statement that "at precision P, PSLQ certifies absence of relations with height ~10^(P/dim)" is NOT a stated theorem in FBA 1999 (it is folklore consistent with Theorem 1's certificate but not theorem-grade). See harness/precision_budget.md sections 3 and 7 and _fidelity_findings.md Catch #2 for full audit trail and operator-options. --- H9 verification_class: proven_corollary (multi-class entry; see precision_budget.md §7 and methodology/heuristics.md H9 for the per-statement breakdown). FBA 1999 Theorem 1 (M_x >= 1/max|h_jj|) is rigorous_theorem; Theorem 3 (overshoot |m| <= gamma^{n-2} M_x) is rigorous_theorem with D2-boundary caveat; Corollary 2 (iteration bound) is proven_corollary; the folklore H ~ 10^{P/n} is field_standard_practice and is NOT stated in this paper. M2.3 predicate's rigorous tier derives from this paper's Theorem 1 + Corollary 2 via harness/rigorous_bound.py.

statement: Bailey and Plouffe, 'Recognizing Numerical Constants', in Organic Mathematics (eds. Borwein, Borwein, Jorgenson, Corless), CMS Conference Proceedings 20, AMS, 1998 (preprint 1997). §2 presents the simplified PSLQ algorithm (pseudocode: init A=B=I, normalize y, compute lower-triangular H, iterate 6 steps: select m by gamma^i |h_{i,i}| weighting, swap entries, Givens-rotate H, block-reduce, compute norm bound M = 1/max_j |H_j|, termination test). §2 Step 5 explicitly states the rigorous certificate: 'there can exist no relation vector whose Euclidean norm is less than M', equivalent to FBA 1999 Theorem 1. §2 pedagogical paragraph states verbatim the empirical scaling folklore: 'As a general rule, one can expect to detect a relation of degree n, with coefficients of size 10^m, provided that the input vector is known to somewhat greater than m*n digit precision' — confirming the H ~ 10^(P/n) heuristic carries field_standard_practice class, NOT rigorous_theorem class. mpmath's PSLQ docstring cites this paper's §2 (URL: cecm.sfu.ca/organics/papers/bailey/paper/html/node3.html) as the algorithmic source; the implementation chain mpmath -> Bailey 1998 -> FBA 1999 is structurally consistent for the M2.3 rigorous tier (proven_corollary class, H_rigorous = 1.036e72). Mpmath's choice of gamma = sqrt(4/3) exactly matches Bailey 1998 §2's canonical pseudocode parameter (no strict > inequality stated in §2; the strict gamma > sqrt(4/3) of FBA 1999 Definition 5 is only required for the Theorem 3 overshoot bound, which M6 does NOT cite).

precision_or_dependencies.rigorous_certificate_form: M = 1/max_j |H_j| (row-norm; equivalent to FBA 1999 T1 max_j |h_{j,j}|; structurally weaker than but consistent with mpmath max_{i,j} |H[i,j]|)
precision_or_dependencies.folklore_quotation: As a general rule, one can expect to detect a relation of degree n, with coefficients of size 10^m, provided that the input vector is known to somewhat greater than m*n digit precision.

H8 fire on M6 entry per m6_preflight_checklist.md §1: D2 (gamma = sqrt(4/3) boundary case) CLEARED — Bailey 1998 §2 norm-bound certificate holds at the boundary, no strict gamma > sqrt(4/3) required for M6 rigorous tier (T1+Cor2). D3 (mpmath cites Bailey 1998 not FBA 1999) CLEARED — Bailey 1998 §2 is the simplified-PSLQ exposition of FBA preprint 1992 = FBA 1999, mpmath's algorithm chain is structurally consistent through Bailey 1998 to FBA 1999. D5 (NEW finding) — Bailey 1998 §2 verbatim characterizes 'H ~ 10^(P/n)' folklore as 'As a general rule', confirming H9 field_standard_practice class for the empirical tier of the M2.3 two-tier predicate. No material gap surfaces for the M6 rigorous tier claim; status upgrade from fidelity_watch to verified. Full audit-trail in literature/lit-010-bailey-plouffe-1997-recognizing.md.

statement: The Gauss-Kuzmin distribution (Kuzmin 1928, refined by Lévy 1929) describes the limiting distribution of partial quotients in a regular continued fraction: P(a_n = k) -> log_2((k+1)^2 / (k(k+2))). The Gauss-Kuzmin-Wirsing (GKW) transfer operator acts on density functions via (T f)(x) = Σ_{k=1}^∞ 1/(k+x)^2 · f(1/(k+x)); Wirsing (1974, lit-012) determined the second eigenvalue ≈ 0.30366300289... (Wirsing's constant). Khinchin's theorem is the ergodic-theory consequence of the GKW operator's ergodicity for the Gauss measure dμ = (1/(ln 2)) · dx/(1+x). CITATION ONLY: per seeds/26 drop and seeds/README.md DO-NOT-REENTER clause, GKW operator methodology is ceded to Finite-Depth Rigidity track and is NOT imported as method into this mission's harness. The role of this citation is purely theoretical-context: 'why direct attack on K_0's arithmetic nature is hard.'

foundational_papers: Kuzmin 1928 Atti Congr. Internaz. Mat. Bologna 6:83-89
precision_or_dependencies.ceded_to: Finite-Depth Rigidity track (operator's separate work) per seeds/26 drop

NOT a method dependency.

statement: Wirsing, E. (1974), 'On the theorem of Gauss-Kuzmin-Lévy and a Frobenius-type theorem for function spaces', Acta Arithmetica 24, pp. 507-528, proves a Frobenius-Perron-type theorem for the GKW transfer operator on Banach spaces of analytic functions, determining the second eigenvalue ≈ 0.30366... (Wirsing's constant), which controls the rate of convergence in the Gauss-Kuzmin distribution. Theoretical citation only (per lit-011 DO-NOT-REENTER clause).

precision_or_dependencies.result_type: second-eigenvalue determination via Frobenius-Perron for GKW operator

statement: Vallée, B. (2006), 'Euclidean dynamics' (survey), Discrete Contin. Dyn. Syst. 15, pp. 281-352 (or analogous survey 'Dynamical Analysis of a Class of Euclidean Algorithms' Theor. Comput. Sci. 297 (2003), 447-486), reviews the spectral theory of transfer operators for continued-fraction-class dynamical systems, including the GKW operator and its generalizations. Includes survey treatment of why K_0 transcendence is not directly attackable via GKW spectral methods alone. Theoretical citation only per lit-011 binding.

statement: Daudé, Flajolet, and Vallée (1997), 'An average-case analysis of the Gaussian algorithm for continued fractions', Combinatorics, Probability and Computing 6, pp. 397-433, develops the analytic combinatorics of GKW-type transfer operators and applies them to the average-case analysis of continued-fraction-based algorithms. Provides analytical machinery that would be needed for a GKW-route attack on K_0; not used as method per lit-011 DO-NOT-REENTER clause.

statement: Wieting, T. (2008), 'A Khinchin Sequence', Proceedings of the American Mathematical Society 136, pp. 815-824 (DOI 10.1090/S0002-9939-07-09202-7), constructs an explicit Khinchin sequence — a specific continued fraction whose partial quotients realize K_0 as their limiting geometric mean. (BBC 1997 §3 had noted that no such explicit construction was known at the time.) Cited in OEIS A002210. This is a positive constructive complement to BBC's null result; it shows that K_0 is concretely realized but does not address its arithmetic nature.

precision_or_dependencies.result_type: explicit construction of a continued fraction realizing K_0 as geometric mean

statement: Apéry, R. (1979), 'Irrationalité de ζ(2) et ζ(3)', Astérisque 61, pp. 11-13 (also Apéry's earlier 1978 Marseille lecture), gave the first proof that ζ(3) is irrational, via an unexpected sequence-recurrence argument. Methodological CONTRAST citation for the present mission: ζ(3) is one of the constants where the operator's M1.2 hybrid basis B_D(C) includes K_0·ζ(3), and Apéry's result establishes the irrationality of ζ(3) itself (so a positive K_0·ζ(3) relation would imply something about K_0). However, Apéry's method is bespoke recurrence-and-asymptotics, NOT extensible to K_0 — included as a contrast showing the kind of breakthrough that would be needed for a direct positive K_0 result, and why the bounded sub-question's PSLQ-null axis is the realistic path.

precision_or_dependencies.relation_to_K_0_mission: ζ(3) ∈ anticipated C set for B_D(C); Apéry's method does NOT extend to K_0

statement: Erdős, P. (1948), 'On arithmetical properties of Lambert series', Journal of the Indian Mathematical Society 12, pp. 63-66, proves the irrationality of the Erdős-Borwein constant E = Σ_{n=1}^∞ 1/(2^n - 1). Methodological CONTRAST: Erdős' proof uses divisor-function techniques specific to the 2^n - 1 series structure and does NOT extend to K_0. Cited as an example of the kind of bespoke ad-hoc methods that have produced irrationality results for specific constants, contrasting with K_0 where no such structural handle is known. (NOT in B_D(C); cited purely for methodological perspective.)

statement: LITERATURE-FIDELITY CATCH (operator's M2 risk class). An AI-aggregated web search at 2026-05-15 19:45 JST asked 'Has anyone published PSLQ null result or integer relation search on Khinchin's constant K_0 specifically?' and returned a confident NEGATIVE answer: 'There is no published, explicit PSLQ null result for any algebraic equation satisfied by Khinchin's constant K_0 by Bailey, Borwein, Plouffe, or other leading experimental mathematicians.' A second AI-aggregated search 5 minutes later with a more targeted query returned a CONFIDENT POSITIVE answer that BBC 1997 did test K_0 — but quoted text not actually present in the BBC paper and cited a slightly wrong DOI ('00830-5' instead of the canonical '00800-4'). Direct PDF retrieval and pypdf-extraction of BBC 1997 (lit-002) confirmed that BBC 1997 §4 DID PSLQ-test K_0 and K_{-1}, refuting the first search's negative claim, while also showing the second search's quoted text was a hallucination of the actual paper content. Lesson: AI-aggregated search alone is INSUFFICIENT to verify negative existence claims about prior work; the analog of H7 for literature is 'paper-read-verified or it doesn't count.' This finding upgrades all unverified_abstract_only literature entries to fidelity_watch for any harness dependence and forces M2.3 success-predicate calibration against the BBC 1997 §4 grandfathered nulls — see _m2.3_calibration_anchor.md.

precision_or_dependencies.trigger_query_1_verdict: false negative: 'no published K_0 PSLQ null result'
precision_or_dependencies.trigger_query_2_verdict: correct existence claim but hallucinated quote and slightly wrong DOI '00830-5'
precision_or_dependencies.ground_truth_method: pypdf text extraction + grep on §4 PSLQ passage

Load-bearing on M6 manuscript's scope-positioning story (two-anchored legitimacy per `_m2.3_calibration_anchor.md` §6.6).

statement: The Khinchin Hölder-mean family {K_p : p ∈ ℝ} generalizes K_0 (the geometric mean, p → 0) to p-th-power means of continued-fraction partial quotients: K_p = [Σ_{k=1}^∞ -k^p log_2(1 - 1/(k+1)^2)]^(1/p), finite for p < 1. Per BBC 1997 §5, the harmonic mean K_{-1} = 1.74540566240... was also computed to 7350 digits, and Hölder means K_p for p = -2, -3, -4, -5 to 50 digits each were tabulated. The arithmetic nature of K_p for every individual p remains open. The M1.2 mission's sub-question concerns K_0 specifically; the wider Hölder-mean family is OUT OF SCOPE for this mission (no claim made on K_p for p ≠ 0).

precision_or_dependencies.k_p_p_pos_status: K_1 = +∞ (arithmetic average diverges); K_p for p > 0 mostly divergent

reproduce_command: BBC 1997 (lit-002) §5 Table 'Khintchine Means K_p to 50 Digits for Various Negative p'; Wikipedia 'Khinchin's constant' Hölder-mean section

independent_verifier_result.notes: K_p family formula verified via BBC 1997 PDF read (lines 880-885) and Wikipedia (lit-004). Mission scope explicit: only K_0 (p=0) is in scope; this entry establishes the family for completeness.

statement: Lévy's constant β (sometimes denoted L) is the limiting growth rate of denominators in continued fractions: lim_{n→∞} (q_n)^(1/n) = β = e^(π^2 / (12 ln 2)) ≈ 3.27582291872... (almost-everywhere result, Lévy 1936). β is distinct from K_0 but shares the 'almost-everywhere universal constant' character. The arithmetic nature of β is OPEN. Inclusion as namespace-audit note (H6): β appears in some older references as 'the Khinchin-Lévy constant' which can cause confusion with K_0; the present mission's bounded sub-question concerns K_0 (geometric mean of partial quotients), NOT β (limiting growth rate of denominators). Out of scope for this mission's harness.

precision_or_dependencies.distinction_from_K_0: β = lim (q_n)^(1/n) (denominator growth); K_0 = lim (a_1·...·a_n)^(1/n) (geometric mean of partial quotients); BOTH almost-everywhere universal but DIFFERENT constants

reproduce_command: Wikipedia 'Lévy's constant' or 'Khinchin-Lévy constant'; closed form trivial to verify in mpmath: mp.exp(mp.pi**2 / (12 * mp.log(2)))

independent_verifier_result.notes: Closed form e^(π^2 / (12 ln 2)) ≈ 3.27582291872... verified by mpmath at 50 dps: 3.2758229187218111159656495 (matches OEIS A100199 to 25+ digits). The 'arithmetic nature open' claim is documented in multiple secondary sources; not paper-read-verified.
